task_completion.description: Verify that the scotus_transcriber directory is created in the correct Talon user folder and contains the required files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer or terminal window showing the path C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\scotus_transcriber, containing 14-556.json, transcriber.talon, and a Python file (.py).

task_completion.description: Verify that the Obergefell v. Hodges metadata was downloaded and saved as 14-556.json.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 14-556.json is visible in an editor or terminal, containing JSON data from the Oyez API, including the case name 'Obergefell v. Hodges'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Python module parses advocate names from the JSON and tracks the active speaker.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python code shows file reading logic for 14-556.json, extraction of advocate names to populate a Talon list (e.g., mod.list()), and a state variable for the active speaker initialized to 'Chief Justice'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Python module defines the required actions for the transcriber.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python code defines Talon actions (@mod.action_class) to: 1) update the speaker, 2) record a statement that formats the output as '[HH:MM] {speaker}: {text}\n' with the text capitalized, and 3) insert the case name.

task_completion.description: Verify that transcriber.talon implements the required voice commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The transcriber.talon file shows voice commands mapped to the Python actions: switching speakers using the advocate list, setting the speaker to 'Chief Justice', recording dictated text, and inserting the case name.

integrity.description: Verify that the Python script dynamically reads and parses the JSON file for advocate names.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code should use json.load() or similar to extract advocates, rather than having a hardcoded list of names for this specific case.

integrity.description: Verify that the JSON file was actually downloaded from the API.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands like curl/wget, a Python script, or a browser downloading from https://api.oyez.org/cases/2014/14-556, rather than the agent manually typing out a fake JSON file.

integrity.description: Verify that the code files were created and edited using a real text editor or IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python and Talon files should be visible in an editor like VS Code, Notepad, or Vim, showing proper formatting and syntax highlighting.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\scotus_transcriber. API URL: https://api.oyez.org/cases/2014/14-556. Case name: Obergefell v. Hodges. The JSON file must be named 14-556.json and contain real API data.
